objective: Introduce a functioning VAT system by Q4 2026, covering 89 mandatory registrants at the $100,000 threshold, with voluntary registration supported and refund risk managed through human-authorised review protocols.
source: IMF (2025), Republic of the Marshall Islands: Technical Assistance Report — Consumption and Income Tax Reform, Recommendations 4.1–4.7
staffContext: Seven staff in a manual operating environment. No integrated tax administration system. VAT would be the first new tax in over a decade. Each recommendation requires dedicated attention from staff already managing existing GRT, wages, and expatriate tax obligations.
generatedBy: Prompt: IMF TA recommendations + Marshall Islands context → Claude (claude-sonnet-4-5) → structured project plan
generatedNote: This plan was generated in approximately 40 seconds by providing the IMF's seven challenge areas, the Marshall Islands operational context, and a structured planning prompt to Claude. It was reviewed and confirmed as realistic by the author. All dates and effort estimates are illustrative.

milestones.name: Project governance established
milestones.description: Long-term advisor appointed, steering committee constituted, project charter signed by Commissioner.

milestones.name: Unified VAT register built
milestones.description: GRT data reconciled into a clean VAT-ready register with consistent fields, validated entries, and voluntary registrant candidates identified.

milestones.name: VAT legislation enacted
milestones.description: VAT Bill drafted, submitted to Attorney General, returned with comments, revised. Awaiting Nitijela passage.

milestones.name: Training materials and SOPs complete
milestones.description: All staff training materials, taxpayer guides, and standard operating procedures finalised and approved by Commissioner.

milestones.name: Staff trained and taxpayers pre-registered
milestones.description: All DCTRT staff trained on VAT procedures. All 89 mandatory registrants issued provisional VAT numbers and briefed on obligations.

milestones.name: VAT go-live
milestones.description: VAT operational. First returns due in January 2027. Refund review protocol active. Monitoring dashboard live.

milestones.name: Post-implementation review complete
milestones.description: First quarter of VAT returns processed. Compliance rate measured against projections. Refund decisions completed with audit trail. ITAS integration assessed.

phases.name: Foundation
phases.label: Ph. 1 — Foundation
phases.summary: Establish governance, recruit advisor, assess capacity, build the taxpayer register, and specify IT requirements. These tasks are prerequisites for all subsequent work.

phases.name: Design and Development
phases.label: Ph. 2 — Design
phases.summary: Draft legislation, design procedures and forms, produce training materials and taxpayer guides, and initiate IT procurement. LLM accelerates drafting across all workstreams simultaneously.

phases.name: Testing and Readiness
phases.label: Ph. 3 — Testing
phases.summary: Deliver staff training, run taxpayer education campaign, complete pre-registration of mandatory registrants, pilot with ten large taxpayers, and conduct end-to-end system testing.

phases.name: Go-Live and Monitoring
phases.label: Ph. 4 — Go-Live
phases.summary: VAT goes live 1 October 2026. First returns due January 2027. Refund review, post-implementation monitoring, and ITAS integration (if delivered) in this phase.

tasks.name: Project governance and long-term advisor
tasks.objective: Establish project governance structure, appoint a steering committee, and secure a long-term IMF/PFTAC-funded advisor with VAT implementation experience to lead the technical workstreams.
outputs: Project charter signed by Commissioner
Advisor terms of reference and contract (PFTAC-funded)
Steering committee mandate and first meeting minutes
High-level project timeline approved
tasks.llmRole: Draft advisor terms of reference from the IMF recommendations, tailored to the Marshall Islands context. Produce project charter template. Generate steering committee agenda and reporting templates. Produce weekly status report template.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner sign-off on charter and TOR. Cabinet endorsement where required. Contract negotiation with advisor. Budget allocation and PFTAC coordination.
tasks.notes: PFTAC-funded advisors typically cost US$200,000–$300,000 per year. The LLM reduces the advisor's time on drafting and administrative templates, concentrating it on technical judgment and stakeholder management.

tasks.name: Workflow and capacity diagnostic
tasks.objective: Map how the DCTRT currently operates — where information flows, where decisions are made, and where capacity bottlenecks sit. This diagnostic determines which LLM use cases are feasible and in what sequence, and informs the staffing and training plan.
outputs: Workflow map covering all six core tax functions
Capacity assessment by staff member and function
Ranked list of LLM use cases by feasibility and expected value
Identified risks and constraints specific to Marshall Islands context
tasks.llmRole: Synthesise interview notes and internal documents into a structured diagnostic report. Generate the workflow map from officer-described processes. Identify capacity gaps against the IMF's ten focus areas. Produce ranked use-case list with rationale.
tasks.humanRequired: Conducting interviews with DCTRT staff. Reviewing outputs for accuracy and cultural appropriateness. Approving the diagnostic findings before they inform subsequent planning.
tasks.notes: The LLM's value here is synthesis speed — turning informal oral knowledge into documented, structured analysis. In administrations where institutional knowledge is entirely oral, this single task may prevent the loss of that knowledge if a key staff member departs.

tasks.name: Unified VAT register from GRT data
tasks.objective: Reconcile the GRT register, business licence register, and VAT interest log into a single structured register with consistent fields, validated entries, and provisional VAT IDs. Identify mandatory registrants (≥$100,000 turnover), voluntary candidates, and data quality gaps.
outputs: Unified VAT register — 112 records with provisional IDs, clean fields, confidence ratings
Data quality flag report — missing contacts, low-confidence turnover, name discrepancies
Voluntary registrant candidate list with motivation notes from officer log
Three flagged refund-risk records with officer review recommendation
tasks.llmRole: Reconcile three inconsistent source files into a unified schema. Flag duplicates, name variations, and missing data. Preserve officer notes as structured fields. Identify voluntary registrant candidates from interest log. Apply risk flags to high-refund-ratio new registrants.
tasks.humanRequired: Officer review of flagged records. Approval of final register before use in registration correspondence. Authorisation of any record deemed a potential duplicate or fraud risk.
tasks.notes: Equivalent to Task 1 in the VAT Register demonstration. The LLM replaces what would otherwise require weeks of manual data entry and reconciliation by a staff member who has other obligations.

tasks.name: ITAS business requirements specification
tasks.objective: Produce a business requirements specification (BRS) for the eventual Integrated Tax Administration System, based on the workflow diagnostic and DCTRT's actual operating needs. This document drives the procurement process and vendor evaluation.
outputs: Business requirements specification (BRS) — functional and non-functional requirements
Prioritised feature list (must-have vs. desirable)
Vendor evaluation criteria
Interim operating model — how to manage with spreadsheets and LLM until ITAS is operational
tasks.llmRole: Draft BRS from workflow diagnostic findings and standard ITAS capability frameworks (COTS systems used in Pacific). Generate vendor evaluation matrix. Produce interim operating model documentation.
tasks.humanRequired: Advisor review and sign-off on BRS. Commissioner approval before release to vendors. Procurement managed through appropriate government channels.
tasks.notes: The ITAS will likely take 18–24 months to procure and implement. The interim operating model — LLM + spreadsheets — is the primary working environment for the entire go-live period.

tasks.name: VAT legislation and regulations
tasks.objective: Draft VAT legislation consistent with the IMF's design recommendations (standard-rate VAT, $100,000 threshold, quarterly filing, broad base with limited exemptions). Prepare for Nitijela passage by February 2026.
outputs: VAT Bill — ready for Nitijela introduction
VAT Regulations — administrative detail below legislation level
Explanatory memorandum in plain language for Nitijela members
Summary guide for taxpayers: what changes, when, and what they must do
tasks.llmRole: Draft initial VAT Bill from IMF model legislation and comparator PIC VAT laws (Fiji, Samoa, Vanuatu). Draft plain-language explanatory memorandum. Produce taxpayer summary. Identify gaps between draft and IMF recommendations for attorney review.
tasks.humanRequired: Attorney General review and sign-off on all legislative drafts. Commissioner approval of policy intent. Nitijela introduction and passage. Political management of any contested provisions (threshold, exemptions).
tasks.notes: Currently in review with the Attorney General's office. Expected Nitijela passage Q1 2026. The LLM produced the initial draft and the explanatory materials; legal review has required substantive revisions to the drafting.

tasks.name: Standard operating procedures
tasks.objective: Produce standard operating procedures (SOPs) covering all routine VAT administration tasks: registration, return processing, assessment, collections, audit, and refunds. SOPs must reflect the DCTRT's seven-staff reality and interim LLM + spreadsheet environment.
outputs: SOP set — eight procedures covering all core VAT workflows
Decision flowcharts for common officer judgment points
Refund review protocol (human-authorised gate for all refunds)
Risk escalation matrix — which issues go to the Commissioner
tasks.llmRole: Draft all eight SOPs from workflow diagnostic, IMF recommendations, and comparator PICs' VAT manuals. Produce decision flowcharts. Draft refund review protocol with explicit human-authorisation gate. Generate risk escalation matrix.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner review and approval of all SOPs. Staff review for operational accuracy. Approval of refund review protocol — this is a high-stakes governance decision about who authorises what.
tasks.notes: SOPs complete and approved. The refund review protocol explicitly prohibits automated refund payment — all refunds require officer sign-off after document verification. This addresses the voluntary registrant fraud risk identified in Task 1.3.

tasks.name: VAT return form and taxpayer guidance
tasks.objective: Design the VAT return form (paper and electronic), produce the accompanying filing guide, and develop a sample invoice template that meets the VAT invoice requirements under the proposed legislation.
outputs: VAT return form — paper version for filing period one
VAT return filing guide — step-by-step for a 89-registrant base
Sample VAT invoice template with mandatory fields labelled
Worked examples — three scenarios covering common registrant situations
tasks.llmRole: Design return form layout based on IMF recommendations and Samoa/Fiji precedent. Draft filing guide in plain English. Produce invoice template with field explanations. Write worked examples covering: mandatory registrant with straightforward sales, mandatory registrant with input credits, voluntary registrant claiming refund.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner approval of form design. Legal review of mandatory invoice fields. Translation into Marshallese (supervised, for official versions).
tasks.notes: Form and guides complete. Marshallese-language versions are under supervised review — the LLM produced an initial Marshallese translation which is being checked by a native-speaking staff member before finalisation.

tasks.name: Staff training curriculum and materials
tasks.objective: Produce a complete staff training programme covering VAT law, administration procedures, the new return form, taxpayer handling, and the interim LLM-assisted workflows. Training must be deliverable by existing staff with advisor support, with no external training contractor required.
outputs: Training curriculum — five modules mapped to staff roles
Facilitator guide — enables advisor or senior officer to deliver training
Participant workbook — exercises, case studies, and reference materials
Assessment tool — brief quiz per module to confirm staff readiness
LLM workflow guide — how to use Claude for routine administration tasks
tasks.llmRole: Draft all five training modules from legislation, SOPs, and return form. Produce facilitator guide with session plans. Generate case studies from the illustrative VAT register (anonymised). Write participant workbook. Produce LLM workflow guide with specific prompts for each common task.
tasks.humanRequired: Advisor review and approval of all training content. Commissioner sign-off before delivery. Staff feedback loop after pilot delivery.
tasks.notes: In progress. Modules 1–3 (VAT law, registration, return filing) are drafted and under advisor review. Modules 4–5 (compliance procedures, LLM workflows) drafting in progress.

tasks.name: Taxpayer education campaign materials
tasks.objective: Develop the full taxpayer education package for the pre-registration campaign (April–June 2026). Materials must explain what VAT is, who must register, what records to keep, how to file, and what invoices look like — at a reading level accessible to all 89 mandatory registrants.
outputs: Plain-language VAT guide — two pages, English and Marshallese
FAQ document — 20 most common questions with direct answers
Registration notice — letter to all 89 mandatory registrants
Social media and radio announcement scripts
Face-to-face workshop outline — for Majuro and outer-island delivery
tasks.llmRole: Draft all materials from legislation and the plain-language return filing guide. Write FAQ drawing on the officer interest log (Source C) for actual questions taxpayers have asked. Produce registration notice as a personalised letter template. Draft workshop outline.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner approval of all public-facing materials. Marshallese translation review by native speaker. Cultural appropriateness check for outer-island communication. Workshop delivery by officer (cannot be delegated to LLM).
tasks.notes: In progress. English-language materials drafted. Marshallese translations in review. Workshop outline complete. Radio scripts pending Commissioner approval.

tasks.name: ITAS procurement process
tasks.objective: Issue a request for proposals for an ITAS using the BRS from Task 1.4, evaluate vendor responses, and make a selection recommendation to Cabinet. The ITAS is a long-term system — its absence does not block go-live in October 2026.
outputs: RFP document — issued to shortlisted vendors
Evaluation matrix and vendor scores
Selection recommendation to Cabinet
Implementation timeline (conditional on Cabinet approval)
tasks.llmRole: Draft RFP from BRS. Produce evaluation matrix and scoring guidance. Draft selection recommendation report. Generate implementation timeline options for Cabinet consideration.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner and Cabinet approval at each stage. Vendor contract negotiation. ADB/PFTAC coordination for funding. Legal review of procurement process.
tasks.notes: RFP issued to three vendors in January 2026. Vendor responses received in February. Evaluation in progress. Selection recommendation expected April 2026. ITAS implementation, if approved, will run parallel to the go-live period and is not a prerequisite for October 2026.

tasks.name: Staff training delivery
tasks.objective: Deliver the five-module staff training programme to all DCTRT staff. All seven staff must complete the programme and pass the readiness assessment before the taxpayer education campaign begins.
outputs: Training completion records — all seven staff
Assessment results — per module, per staff member
Training debrief report — gaps identified and remediation plan
Updated training materials (incorporating staff feedback)
tasks.llmRole: Generate additional case studies and exercises if gaps are identified during delivery. Produce remediation materials for staff who require additional support. Update training materials based on delivery feedback.
tasks.humanRequired: Advisor delivers all training sessions in person. Commissioner participates in Module 1 (VAT law and policy intent). All staff complete assessments. Commissioner reviews results and approves readiness.
tasks.notes: Delivery requires in-person sessions in Majuro. Outer-island staff may require video delivery or printed materials. Advisor to confirm logistics in April 2026.

tasks.name: Taxpayer education campaign
tasks.objective: Execute the taxpayer education campaign. All 89 mandatory registrants must receive a registration notice, have access to the taxpayer guide, and understand their obligations before pre-registration begins in May 2026.
outputs: Registration notices sent to all 89 mandatory registrants
Campaign report — reach, attendance at workshops, queries received
FAQ update — new questions arising during campaign added and published
Pre-registration enquiry log — businesses expressing interest in voluntary registration
tasks.llmRole: Generate personalised registration notices from the VAT register (Task 1.3). Draft responses to taxpayer queries received during campaign. Update FAQ with new questions. Maintain pre-registration enquiry log.
tasks.humanRequired: Officer delivers face-to-face workshops. Officer handles taxpayer queries (LLM can draft responses for officer review). Commissioner approves any non-standard communications.
tasks.notes: Campaign design allows for radio broadcast, print materials, and workshops. Outer-island reach may require coordination with local government contacts.

tasks.name: Mandatory registrant pre-registration
tasks.objective: Complete pre-registration of all 89 mandatory registrants. Issue final VAT registration certificates. Process voluntary registration applications (expected 15–25). Update the VAT register with confirmed registration status and final contact details.
outputs: VAT registration certificates — issued to all confirmed registrants
Final VAT register — updated with confirmed status, contacts, and voluntary registrations
Voluntary registrant risk assessment — refund-flagged records reviewed before registration confirmed
Non-registrant list — businesses contacted but not yet registered, with follow-up plan
tasks.llmRole: Generate registration certificates from register template. Identify voluntary registrants whose application notes suggest high early-refund risk. Produce risk assessment summary for officer review. Update register with confirmed data.
tasks.humanRequired: Officer reviews all voluntary registrant applications before approval. Commissioner authorises registration of any flagged high-risk applicants. Officer follows up with non-responding mandatory registrants.
tasks.notes: The refund-risk assessment for voluntary registrants is the critical human-in-the-loop point in this task. The LLM flags; the officer decides.

tasks.name: Pilot with ten largest mandatory registrants
tasks.objective: Run a filing simulation with the ten largest mandatory registrants. Each business completes a practice Q3 2026 return, and the DCTRT processes it through the full workflow. Identify procedural gaps before go-live.
outputs: Ten completed practice returns — processed end-to-end
Pilot debrief report — issues identified, SOPs requiring revision
Updated SOPs (where pilot identified gaps)
Registrant feedback — simplified as a short survey
tasks.llmRole: Generate synthetic practice return data for each of the ten pilot registrants. Process returns through the SOP workflow and flag anomalies. Draft pilot debrief report. Identify SOP sections requiring revision based on pilot findings.
tasks.humanRequired: Advisor facilitates pilot sessions with each registrant. Officer processes returns. Commissioner reviews debrief report and approves SOP revisions before go-live.
tasks.notes: Pilot may reveal that the return form needs simplification or that the SOP for handling errors requires additional officer guidance. The debrief report is the critical input to Task 3.5.

tasks.name: End-to-end system and compliance test
tasks.objective: Confirm that the end-to-end system — register, return processing, assessment, collections, refund review, and monitoring — is operational and tested before go-live on 1 October 2026.
outputs: Go-live readiness assessment — signed by Commissioner
Final register — all 89+ registrants confirmed and notified
Monitoring dashboard — filing rate, payment compliance, refund queue
Refund review queue — any pre-received refund applications reviewed and decided before go-live
tasks.llmRole: Run test queries against the final register. Generate monitoring dashboard template. Process any pre-received refund applications through the risk assessment workflow for officer review. Produce go-live readiness checklist.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner signs off on go-live readiness. Officer confirms all registrants notified. Any refund decisions made by authorised officer before go-live.
tasks.notes: This task is Commissioner-routed because the go-live decision carries full institutional responsibility. The LLM produces the readiness evidence; the Commissioner makes the call.

tasks.name: VAT go-live and Q1 taxpayer support
tasks.objective: Operate the VAT system through the first full quarter (October–December 2026). First returns are due in January 2027. Provide active taxpayer support throughout Q1.
outputs: VAT operational — all 89+ registrants active
Taxpayer support log — queries received and responses provided
Compliance monitoring report — weekly filing rate and payment status
Q1 return preparation guide — distributed to all registrants in December 2026
tasks.llmRole: Draft responses to taxpayer queries (for officer review and send). Maintain compliance monitoring dashboard. Generate weekly status reports for Commissioner. Produce Q1 return preparation guide.
tasks.humanRequired: Officers handle all taxpayer interactions. All query responses reviewed before sending. Commissioner receives weekly status briefings.
tasks.notes: This is the highest-volume support period. The LLM handles drafting and monitoring; the officer handles all taxpayer contact and decisions.

tasks.name: First return processing and refund review
tasks.objective: Process all Q1 2026 VAT returns. Assess all refund claims — each requiring officer sign-off before payment. Identify and follow up with non-filers. Produce the first compliance statistics report.
outputs: All Q1 returns processed and assessed
Refund decisions — each with officer authorisation and audit trail
Non-filer follow-up — written notices issued
Q1 compliance statistics report — filing rate, payment rate, refund total, non-filer count
tasks.llmRole: Process returns through SOP workflow. Generate refund risk assessments for officer review. Draft non-filer notices. Produce Q1 compliance statistics report.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner authorises all refund payments — this is a mandatory human gate. Officer issues all enforcement notices. Commissioner reviews Q1 statistics and determines whether threshold, rate, or compliance approach requires adjustment.
tasks.notes: The refund authorisation gate is the most important human-in-the-loop requirement in the entire system. No automated payment of any refund. The LLM flags and assesses; the Commissioner signs.

tasks.name: Post-implementation monitoring and reporting
tasks.objective: Maintain a live monitoring system tracking VAT performance against projections through the post-implementation period. Generate monthly reports for the Commissioner and quarterly reports for PFTAC/IMF.
outputs: Monthly monitoring reports — filing rate, payment compliance, revenue vs. projection, refund queue
Anomaly alerts — automated flag when a metric moves outside expected range
Q1 post-implementation report — for Commissioner and PFTAC
Lessons learned register — issues arising and how they were resolved
tasks.llmRole: Build and maintain monitoring dashboard from spreadsheet inputs. Generate monthly narrative reports from data. Flag anomalies with plain-language explanation and recommended action. Produce post-implementation report in PFTAC format.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner interprets anomalies and decides whether they indicate fraud, confusion, or a policy problem. PFTAC review of quarterly report. Decisions on threshold adjustment, exemptions, or enforcement escalation.
tasks.notes: This task runs concurrently with 4.1 and 4.2. The LLM is the reporting engine; the Commissioner makes all interpretive decisions.

tasks.name: ITAS integration (conditional on procurement outcome)
tasks.objective: If the ITAS is delivered in time, migrate the VAT register and Q1 returns data into the system and confirm operational handover. If not, extend the LLM + spreadsheet operating model and revise the ITAS timeline.
outputs: ITAS go-live (if delivered) — with data migration confirmed
Parallel operation report — confirming ITAS matches LLM + spreadsheet outputs
Revised ITAS timeline (if not delivered by March 2027)
Operational handover plan — transitioning daily workflows to ITAS
tasks.llmRole: Draft data migration specification and test plan. Generate parallel operation comparison report. Produce revised ITAS timeline if required. Draft operational handover plan.
tasks.humanRequired: Commissioner authorises data migration. Vendor manages ITAS implementation. PFTAC/ADB coordination for ongoing funding if timeline extends.
tasks.notes: This task is conditional. October 2026 go-live is designed to be fully independent of the ITAS. If the ITAS is not operational by March 2027, the LLM + spreadsheet model continues as the primary operating environment.
